tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: At 50, Ultramarathoner Defies Age to Win His Third Consecutive Hardrock 100
stories.summary: Fifty-year-old Ludovic Pommeret, a French trail runner, just set the fastest time ever on Colorado's Hardrock 100, breaking his own record by 20 minutes in his third consecutive victory.

stories.headline: One Hour, 25,000 Volunteers, 361,000 Trees: Gujarat Breaks World Record for Community Reforestation
stories.summary: In a breathtaking display of collective action, volunteers across Gandhinagar planted a forest's worth of trees in 60 minutes, setting a Guinness world record.

stories.headline: Three Generations of One Family's 30-Year Mission: Helping the Homeless Find Home
stories.summary: Minnie McNeil, 85, and three generations of her family have helped Chester County reach functional zero for chronic homelessness through 30+ years of dedicated service.

stories.headline: From Visiting Food Pantries to Running 20: A Woman's Quiet Revolution in Dignity
stories.summary: Alyssa Curtis, who once relied on food pantries herself, now runs 20 judgment-free community pantries across Michigan, serving neighbors with grace and respect.

stories.headline: Australian Numbat Returns from the Brink: 18 Years of Dedication Pay Off
stories.summary: Australia's numbat population has rebounded from just 300 in the late 1970s to between 2,000 and 3,000 today, earning removal from the endangered species list after 15 years of conservation work.

stories.headline: San Francisco Neighbors Unite: One City Day Brings 170 Projects and Thousands Together
stories.summary: Thousands of San Francisco neighbors gathered for One City Day, completing 170 projects from tree planting to mural painting, rekindling their love for the city they share.

stories.headline: At 21, Linda Noskova Overcomes Five Match Points to Claim Her First Grand Slam at Wimbledon
stories.summary: Twenty-one-year-old Linda Noskova overcame five match points and a shattered second set to win her first Grand Slam at Wimbledon, claiming the Venus Rosewater Dish in an all-Czech final.

stories.headline: A Dubai Engineer's Random Acts of Kindness: 240 Meals for Delivery Riders
stories.summary: Tarik Toptas, a 24-year-old Turkish software engineer in Dubai, has donated 240 meals to delivery riders in just two weeks, planning to give away 500 more.

stories.headline: Solar Panels in the Desert Create a Living Skin That Stops Desertification
stories.summary: Arizona researchers discovered that solar panels foster thriving biocrust beneath them, tripling microbial life while fighting climate change—nature and clean energy in harmony.

stories.headline: When a Wedding Cake Emergency Became a Story of Community Spirit
stories.summary: Baker Elria Billson stepped in Friday evening to rescue a wedding cake crisis, guiding a family's baker and her 81-year-old great-grandmother to create stunning cakes by 21:00.

stories.headline: History at the Range: India's First Woman Trap Shooter Wins Gold at World Cup
stories.summary: Twenty-six-year-old Neeru Dhanda became India's first woman trap shooter to win ISSF World Cup gold, breaking the national record with a 121/125 qualification score in Lonato.

stories.headline: In Space, Scientists Bioprint Human Tissue for the First Time—Hope for Organ Transplants Ahead
stories.summary: Scientists aboard the International Space Station successfully bioprinted kidney, liver, and cartilage tissue for the first time, plus 28 nerve repair implants, marking a breakthrough for regenerative medicine.

stories.headline: Thirty-Five Years of Love: 37 Western Pond Turtles Released to Thrive Again
stories.summary: Washington released 37 western pond turtles after 35 years of conservation efforts that have head-started more than 2,300 turtles and brought the endangered species back from the brink.
